name: Pancakes (Crocker)
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Shortening, melted or vegetable oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the egg with a hand beater until fluffy.
2. Add the remaining ingredients to the egg mixture and beat until smooth.
- For thinner pancakes, stir in an additional 1/4 cup milk.
3. Grease a heated griddle if necessary. (To test the griddle, sprinkle a few drops of water on it. If bubbles form around the edges, the heat is just right.)
4. For each pancake, pour about 3 tablespoons batter from the tip of a large spoon or pitcher onto the hot griddle.
5. Cook the pancakes until they are puffed and dry around the edges.
6. Turn the pancakes over and cook the other side until golden brown.
7. Serve 9 (4-inch) pancakes; each pancake contains approximately 100 calories.
Variations:
- Blueberry Pancakes: Stir in 1/2 cup of fresh or frozen blueberries (thawed and well drained).
- Buckwheat Pancakes: Substitute 1/2 cup buckwheat flour and 1/2 cup whole wheat flour for the all-purpose flour. Use 1 cup milk. If desired, sprinkle 1 teaspoon of whole bran or wheat germ over each pancake just before turning.
- Buttermilk Pancakes: Substitute 1 cup buttermilk for the milk. Decrease baking powder to 1 teaspoon and beat in 1/2 teaspoon baking soda.
- Ham Pancakes: Omit sugar. Stir in 1/3 to 1/2 cup of ground or cut-up fully cooked smoked ham.
- Whole Wheat Pancakes: Substitute whole wheat flour for the all-purpose flour and packed brown sugar for the granulated sugar. If using self-rising flour, omit baking powder and salt.
